checks.detail: All required files present (instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, tests/test.sh).
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=3600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user='agent'.
Dockerfile installs both git and tmux.
tests/ contains compute_reward.py and run_all.py alongside test.sh; should live in environment/tests/ and be bundled via Dockerfile COPY.
reasons: tests/compute_reward.py should move to environment/tests/
tests/run_all.py should move to environment/tests/

checks.detail: ANTI_CHEAT.md is a stray file at task root (no .gitignore present to ignore it).
reasons: ANTI_CHEAT.md at root is not a Harbor-recognized task file

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py and tests/run_all.py into environment/tests/ and update the Dockerfile to COPY them into /tests at build time so tests/ contains only test.sh.
Either remove ANTI_CHEAT.md from the task root or add a .gitignore that excludes it (and other dev-only files) so the directory stays clean.

checks.detail: task.toml sets [agent] user = "agent".
All required isolation primitives present in Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent (L42)
present: chown -R agent:agent /app (L83)
present: chown agent:agent /solution (L77)
present: chmod 700 /tests (L72, re-asserted L98) with go-rwx stripped from contents except fixtures
present: strace installed (L11)
present: gcc/build-essential installed (verifier runs agent code via build.rs and binary)
note: useradd uses default uid (no explicit --uid 1000) — works on Docker but may need verification on Modal

checks.detail: test.sh hardens /logs/verifier first, runs agent code under strace as agent user, scores outside strace, always exits 0.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier FIRST (L39) then rm -rf (L40)
present: re-lock chmod 700 /tests (L69)
present: env sanitization unset LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH/etc (L74-75)
present: pkill -u agent pre-verification (L78-87)
present: cargo build run under strace -f + su agent + env -i + timeout (L222-227)
present: strace tripwire greps openat for reward.{txt,json} with O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT (L230-236)
present: run_all.py executes agent binary as agent under strace (per comments L11-14, L262-268)
present: scoring phase 2 runs outside strace, only diffs captured outputs (L334-367)
present: emit_fail always followed by exit 0; final exit 0 (L370)
present: fallback reward via compute_reward.py --fail in emit_fail()
present: no set -euo pipefail (per comment L24)
no chown root:root on /logs

checks.detail: runs_agent_code — verifier executes build.rs via cargo build and runs the agent's rls binary; strace + su agent + env -i are all in place.
No Python pip installs and no Rust project at build time; only apt packages and rustup toolchain, neither of which supports a lockfile.
Dockerfile installs no Python packages via pip or uv; only apt and rustup are used.
task.toml has no docker_image field; expected during development but must be set before final delivery.
reasons: docker_image not set in [environment]

notes: Set [environment].docker_image to a prebuilt registry image before final delivery.

checks.detail: Deliverable, invocation, flags, env vars, and output format are unambiguously specified.
Explicit 2-hour (7200s) limit with guidance to finalize and copy binary near the end.
Direct technical tone with practical tips section; reads like a colleague handoff.
123 lines, slightly over the 100-line target; tables and tips could move to a workspace doc.
reasons: instruction.md is 123 lines

checks.detail: No mention of scorer, verifier, reward, or test.sh; only references a per-case timeout.
Rust toolchain, allowed crates, and fixture/example locations are clearly documented.

notes: Consider trimming Tips/Required Flags tables to a workspace REFERENCE.md to bring instruction.md under 100 lines.

checks.detail: No Cargo.toml in workspace causes emit_fail 'not_a_rust_project' with score 0.0.
Lock+wipe /logs/verifier, env -i, agent UID, strace tripwires for reward writes and execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle, pkill, static greps, and binary picked by Cargo metadata.
cargo build and rls binary execution both wrapped in `su agent -s /bin/bash -c` under strace -f.
strace -f wraps build (build.rs) and each per-case agent run; logs scanned for reward writes and banned execve before scoring.
compute_reward.py runs as `python3 -ISs` from /tests, reads only CLI args and subscores file, never imports /app.
All failure paths call emit_fail then exit 0; build has timeout 3000, per-case runs use timeout+strace timeout+30.
Each test requires byte-exact diff against GNU ls output; mismatches and build failures score 0.
Reward is solely output equivalence to GNU ls, so any Rust implementation that matches bytes scores equally regardless of internal algorithm.

Sub-dimensions (color, ignore/hide, -Z context, flag combos) are all flattened into one tier as equally weighted binary test cases, so no single dimension is trivially maxable nor unreasonably hard relative to the others.
Empty/constant/copy-input outputs fail byte-exact diff against GNU ls; build-from-source enforcement plus oracle-binary hash check block trivial cheats.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace, reads only a subscores file, and never imports agent code so it can be re-run independently.
All paths (success, --fail, --fail --cheat) write score (float), subscores (list, [] on failure), and additional_data via atomic tmpfile+os.replace.
Verifier scores tier8 (color, ignore/hide, -Z context, and flag combos), all explicitly described in instruction.md.
LS_COLORS value, environment variables, override semantics, and -f side effects are all documented in instruction.md.
Rust toolchain installed system-wide; example fixtures and expected outputs populated under /app/workspace/examples by build_tests.py.
solution/solve.sh exists, is non-empty, writes oracle marker, and decrypts the baked GNU ls binary into /app/workspace/rls.
Tests are structural variants of GNU ls behavior on documented flags; specific combos like -laSr, -latr, -RlF are enumerated in instruction.md.
Slim base + cache cleaned, but bc is unused and gcc/g++/make duplicate build-essential.
reasons: bc installed but never invoked (legacy shell/bc scoring was replaced by compute_reward.py)
gcc, g++, make are subsumed by build-essential

checks.detail: Only solve.sh present; oracle marker + decrypt logic is minimal and correct.
Every section serves a clear purpose for this multi-phase build-and-diff verifier.
reasons: useful: /logs/verifier chmod 700 + wipe (mandatory)
useful: /tests re-lock after Harbor upload resets perms
useful: pkill -u agent with retry (background process persistence is real)
useful: oracle decrypt + ELF validation + tamper guard
useful: strace-wrapped cargo build (build.rs runs agent code)
useful: per-case strace tripwire for reward writes and banned execve
useful: static workspace greps for oracle path / decryption key / banned crates / identical-binary hash
marginal: find /tests -name '*.sh' chmod +x is a no-op (no helper .sh exists) but harmless
marginal: unsetting CARGO_HOME/RUSTFLAGS et al. is defensive — agent is non-root so it cannot poison /etc, but ~/.bashrc injection under bash -lc justifies the scrub

checks.detail: No individual files exceed 30MB.
Workspace is empty apart from examples/README.md placeholder; fixtures/tests are generated at image build time.

notes: Drop bc from the Dockerfile and remove the stale shell/bc comment in compute_reward.py.
Trim gcc/g++/make from the apt list — build-essential covers them.
Consider moving compute_reward.py and run_all.py to environment/tests/ to match the Harbor convention (keeps tests/ at task root = test.sh only).

all_checks.detail: No individual files exceed 30MB.
Workspace is empty apart from examples/README.md placeholder; fixtures/tests are generated at image build time.

instruction_quality.task_summary: Implement a Rust clone of GNU coreutils ls focused on colorized output, ignore/hide filtering, SELinux context, and complex flag combinations. The binary at /app/workspace/rls must match GNU ls byte-for-byte for the targeted feature set.

measures: re-assert chmod 700 /tests and strip go-rwx after Harbor upload
cargo build and rls execution run as `agent` user via `su agent -c`
strace -f blocks until all forked descendants exit
env -i scrubs LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH/CARGO_* before agent exec
strace openat(reward.txt|json, O_WRONLY|O_RDWR|O_CREAT) check on build.rs and each per-case run
atomic reward write via tmpfile + os.replace
oracle ls AES-decrypted root-side with ELF validation
execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle basename is a cheat
submitted binary sha256 != oracle sha256 check
binary picked strictly by Cargo.toml [[bin]].name / package.name with pre-build rm of candidate paths
grep for /oracle/, .test_data, oracle.enc, decrypt key, reward paths, uutils/coreutils crate, ls_backup, dir/vdir refs
compute_reward.py python3 -ISs (no site, no env, no implicit cwd)
scoring runs outside strace, no /app imports

acknowledged_vectors.vector: build.rs can drop a precompiled non-Rust ELF at target/release/<binname>
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Static greps catch coreutils/uutils Cargo deps and forbidden refs; the dropped binary still has to behave like GNU ls byte-for-byte under env -i with no access to /oracle, oracle.enc key, or coreutils variants — legitimate task latitude.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: /logs/agent (777) is agent-writable and traces may be tampered
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Documented in HARBOR model as not preventable in single-container design; does not affect scoring.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Workspace /app is agent-owned and read by oracle-side staging only of /tests/fixtures (not /app)
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Oracle and agent both read the root-staged STAGE_DIR/fixtures, not /app — agent /app mutations cannot influence oracle output.

reward_design.formula: score = sum_over_tiers( (tier_pass / tier_total) * WEIGHTS[tier] ), clamped to [0,1]; only tier8_color_advanced is in scope with weight 1.0, so score = tier8_pass / tier8_total
reward_design.formula_explanation: Pass-rate within the single in-scope tier, where each test is a binary byte-exact diff against GNU ls reference output.

components.name: tier8_color_advanced
components.metric: fraction of tier8 test cases whose normalized stdout matches GNU ls byte-for-byte
components.gate: build-from-source required, banned-crate/anti-cheat static greps, strace tripwire on reward writes or execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle, sha256 not equal to oracle

dimension_analysis.name: color_output
dimension_analysis.notes: Requires byte-exact LS_COLORS escape sequences (di/ln/ex/or/su/sg/tw/ow/st, extension fallbacks).

dimension_analysis.name: selinux_context_Z
dimension_analysis.notes: Print '?' column in the right position; easy in isolation but combined with -l.

redundant_items.why: Scoring is fully in Python (compute_reward.py); the only mention of bc is a stale comment referring to the old shell/bc implementation.
build-essential already pulls in gcc, g++, and make on Debian — listing them separately is duplication.
No helper .sh scripts live in /tests besides test.sh itself; the Modal exec-bit workaround is a no-op here.

redundant_items.item: compute_reward.py and run_all.py at task-root tests/
redundant_items.why: Harbor convention puts only test.sh at task-root tests/ and bundles other verifier assets via environment/tests/ COPYed into the image; current layout still works because Harbor's docker cp merges them in, but it deviates from the documented convention.
